name: OmopDiagnosticReportNoteView
title: DiagnosticReport → note (FHIR-flat, stage 1)
description:
When a DiagnosticReport carries free-text content -- either a conclusion string or one or more presentedForm attachments -- a note row is created to preserve the unstructured clinical narrative. This is independent of the domain-routed rows (measurement, observation, procedure_occurrence) that are produced from the report's LOINC code and conclusionCode. A single DiagnosticReport may produce both a domain-routed row and a note row. No reference implementation currently maps DiagnosticReport.conclusion/presentedForm to the OMOP note table; omoponfhir's DocumentReference mapper is the closest reference.

Stage 1 of FHIR→OMOP: produce a flat row keeping source codes per vocabulary. Stage 2 joins vocab.concept to resolve concept_ids.

column.name: id
column.description: FHIR DiagnosticReport.id; surrogate OMOP key derived (hash/sequence) downstream.

extension.valueString: surrogate key from hash/sequence/lookup of FHIR id
lookup vocab.concept WHERE vocabulary_id='LOINC' AND concept_code = (this column); standard_concept='S' preferred
copy text verbatim into OMOP source_value column

column.name: subject_id
column.description: Resolve Patient/{id} reference to integer person_id. Skip row if unresolvable.

extension.valueString: resolve PERSON reference; downstream maps to person_id

column.name: note_date
column.description: Date component of effectiveDateTime, or effectivePeriod.start. If absent, fall back to DiagnosticReport.issued (instant). Skip resource if no date available.

column.name: note_datetime
column.description: Full timestamp from effectiveDateTime, effectivePeriod.start, or issued. Set time to midnight if not given.

column.name: note_class
column.description: Maps the report category to a LOINC Document Type concept per OMOP CDM specification. Map LAB -> 44814645 (Note), RAD -> 44814641 (Radiology report), PAT -> 44814642 (Pathology report). 0 if unmappable.

extension.valueString: resolve CONCEPT reference; downstream maps to note_class_concept_id

column.name: note_title
column.description: Display text of the report's primary code (typically LOINC). E.g. 'History and physical note'. Truncate to 250 characters. Fall back to DiagnosticReport.code.text or the LOINC code itself.

column.name: note_text
column.description: For conclusion: use the string directly. For presentedForm: base64-decode the data field. Only text/plain content types stored directly; other types require extraction or conversion. If both exist, create separate note rows or concatenate.

column.name: encoding
column.description: 32678 = UTF-8. FHIR strings are always UTF-8. For presentedForm, check contentType and encoding; default to 32678 for text/plain.

extension.valueString: resolve CONCEPT reference; downstream maps to encoding_concept_id

column.name: language
column.description: Map BCP-47/ISO 639 language codes to OMOP concepts descended from 4182347 (World Languages). en/en-US -> 4180186, de -> 4182948, fr -> 4181536, es -> 4182511. Fall back to DiagnosticReport.language. 0 if absent.

extension.valueString: resolve CONCEPT reference; downstream maps to language_concept_id

column.name: performer_id
column.description: First performer reference resolved to provider_id. Filter to Practitioner-typed references. resultsInterpreter[0] is an alternative source.

extension.valueString: resolve PROVIDER reference; downstream maps to provider_id

column.name: encounter_id
column.description: Resolve Encounter/{id} to visit_occurrence_id. If unresolvable, log warning but still create the note row with null.

extension.valueString: resolve VISIT_OCCURRENCE reference; downstream maps to visit_occurrence_id
